rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD E. ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Distribtion To Heir Renella Anderson
rulings.Rulings: OFF CALENDAR, although the 13100 affidavit signed by Schynell Strong did not have a certified copy of the death certificate attached to it. A Court-appointed fiduciary, not her attorney, is required to sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment or declaration clarifying identities of heirs. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/11/24 for an appearance for the Court to determine if remaining defects and inconsistent or confusing allegations may be waived. 1. The "second supplemental" was filed on 7/11/24. Notice was mailed on 7/9/24. [LRSF 14.14 A.] 2. That notice mailed on 7/9/24 was apparently for the 7/15/24 hearing, which was not at least 15 days' notice. 3. The instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice for the 7/15/24 hearing still are NOT the correct, current instructions. Counsel should review the court's website, where a link to the instructions is clearly set forth on the Probate Division's page. 4. The executor appears to be uncertain about who the beneficiaries of James Y. Chew's trust are, although he is the successor trustee. 5. The order distributing Frank Chew's estate to this estate showed $1,000.15 and $293,745.74 in cash on hand. The I&A filed in this estate on 12/3/19 reported $293,735.78 in cash, not $293,745.74. 6. If there had been $293,745.74, after payment of the attorney fees and costs and taxes to the US Treasury and FTB, there should have been $165,544.53, not $165,724.53. 7. Exhibit C attached to the "second supplemental" filed on 7/11/24 is not an "amendment" (of what?); it is a Nomination of Executor signed on 6/23/24 by Yuan Miao Wu, although the executor was already appointed almost four years ago. (The "first petition filed in this case" (the petition for probate filed on 6/23/20) WAS signed by Ming Yu Wu.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE AUSTIN EDWARD HILLS 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Successor Trustee?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: (part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Respondent Justin Hills' (hereafter "Respondent") Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. The motion is GRANTED, and Petitioner Austin Hills (hereafter "Petitioner" or "Settlor") is hereby granted leave to amend. Respondent brings the instant Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings seeking a judgment dismissing the first claim asserted in Petitioner's underlying Petition: (1) to Confirm Validity of First Amendment; (2) to Compel Accounting and production of Trust Records; (3) for Redress of Breach of Trust and Surcharge; and (4) for an Order Enjoining Respondent from Using Trust Assets, filed April 29, 2024, (hereafter the "Petition") on the ground that the first claim does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. The Court grants Respondent's Request for Judicial Notice that the Superior Court for the County of San Francisco has not entered an order, in this or any other action, determining that Petitioner Austin Edward Hills is legally competent to act pursuant to Section 3.4. of the Austin Edwards Hills 2004 Trust, as restated on May 26, 2022 (the "Trust"). See Cal. Evid. Code sec. 452(d); Code Civ. Proc. sec. 438(d).) In ruling on a motion for judgment on the pleadings, except as provided by Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 438, the rules governing demurrers apply. (Cloud v. Northrop Grumman Corp. (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 995, 999.) The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings must appear on the face of the challenged pleading or be based on facts the court may judicially notice. (Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 438.) The facts set forth in the pleading under attack must be accepted as true. (Gerawan Farming, Inc. v. Lyons (2000) 24 Cal.App.4th 468, 515-516.) Contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law are disregarded when analyzing whether judgment on the pleadings is warranted. (Vichy Springs Resort, Inc. v. City of Ukiah (2024), 101 Cal.App.5th 46, 53.) Moreover, the court may consider written instruments attached to the challenged pleading and incorporated into it by reference. (Columbia Cas. Co. v. Northwestern Nat'l. Ins. Co. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 457, 468.) It is undisputed that, on February 5, 2024, Respondent exercised the authority granted to him by Provision 3.4 of the Trust and declared Settlor to be incapacitated. (Petition at p. 3:7-12.) It is also undisputed that on April 16, 2024, Settlor executed the First Amendment to his Trust, appointing himself and Debra Dolch as co-trustees of the Trust, revoking provision 3.4, and amending provision 9.1. (Id. at p. 4:9-16.) Notably, during a period of his incapacity, Provision 3.4 explicitly restricts Settlor's authority to amend or revoke the Trust, unless and until a subsequent declaration of capacity is issued by Respondent, or a court of competent jurisdiction has determined Settlor is competent to act. (Id., Exhibit A.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE AUSTIN EDWARD HILLS 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 2) The Petitioner makes three allegations relevant to the instant motion: (1) Respondent improperly declared Petitioner to be incapacitated (Petition at p. 2:7-8); (2) Petitioner is not incapacitated (Id. at p. 3:13); and (3) the Trust neither sets forth an exclusive method of modification nor does it preclude the use of the revocation procedures for purposes of modification under the Probate Code (Id. at p. 5:14-16). This court finds that Petitioner's allegations amount to either contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. Moreover, Petitioner has not pled sufficient facts to establish a claim for relief as to his first cause of action because Petitioner has not alleged that the declaration executed by Respondent on February 5, 2024 is invalid, or alleged that Respondent lacked the authority to exercise the power granted to him by Provision 3.4. Therefore, Respondent's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED as to Petitioner's first cause of action, and Petitioner is hereby granted leave to amend. (See Mendoza v. Rast Produce Co., Inc., (2006) 140 Cal. App. 4th 1395, 1402 ["When a cure is a reasonable possibility, the trial court abuses its discretion by not granting leave to amend"].) The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CRYSTAL LYNN HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication of notice of the AMENDED petition has been filed. (2) No proof of service of notice of the AMENDED petition has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required to be filed, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (5) Is the petitioner the only issue of the predeceased brother, Russell Hicks? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/21/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WALTER C.Y. CHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 9/8/25. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.
